content.problemStatement:
You are in charge of organizing a drag racing championship. The competition will be held at a large empty field, which can be modelled as an infinite plane. The most important thing is to take a lot of cool photos for the media, so you've identified nnn points in the plane with the most beautiful scenery. Each of these points has to be a midpoint of a separate track, and each track has to be aligned with one of cardinal directions (either horizontal or vertical). Of course, no tracks may have a common point due to obvious safety reasons. Finally, all tracks should have equal length 2d2d2d for some integer ddd so that all competitors are in equal conditions.

You now want to find the largest length of the tracks so that directions can be chosen for each track without any possible collision.

###Input:

The first line of input contains TTT, i.e. number of test cases per file.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer nnn ‚àí-‚àí the number of tracks (1‚â§n‚â§100‚Äâ0001 \leq n \leq 100\,0001‚â§n‚â§100000).

Each of the next nnn lines contains two integers xix_ixi‚Äã and yi‚àíy_i-yi‚Äã‚àí midpoint of the respective track (0‚â§xi,yi‚â§1090 \leq x_i, y_i \leq 10^90‚â§xi‚Äã,yi‚Äã‚â§109). All nnn points are distinct.

###Output:
On the first line print a single integer ‚àí-‚àí the largest non-negative number ddd such that directions of tracks of length 2d2d2d with given midpoints can be chosen without any collisions, or ‚àí1-1‚àí1 if ddd can be arbitrarily large. On the following line print a string of nnn characters `|` and `-`, denoting directions of the tracks in the same order as given in the input.

###Constraints


1‚â§T‚â§51  \leq  T  \leq  51‚â§T‚â§5
1‚â§n‚â§100‚Äâ0001 \leq n \leq 100\,0001‚â§n‚â§100000
0‚â§xi,yi‚â§1090 \leq x_i, y_i \leq 10^90‚â§xi‚Äã,yi‚Äã‚â§109
**All nnn points are distinct.**
###Sample Input:

`3
4
0 0
2 0
0 2
2 2
3
0 0
1 0
0 1
3
0 0
1 0
2 2
`###Sample Output:

`1
-||-
0
---
-1
|||
`
